DECLARATION OF DANIEL R. HYDE, III IN SUPPORT OF MOTION OF DEFENDANT DANIEL R. HYDE, III TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS BASED ON LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION (TRANSACTION ID # 63145869) FILED BY DEFENDANT HYDE III, DANIEL R.
MINI MINUTES FOR MAY-01-2019 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
ORDER CONTINUING DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO QUASH (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DANIEL HYDE'S MOTION OF DANIEL R. HYDE, III TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS BASED ON LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION. DEFENDANT DANIEL R. HYDE, III'S MOTION TO QUASH IS CONTINUED TO AUGUST 19, 2019 TO ALLOW FOR JURISDICTIONAL DISCOVERY. IT IS PLAINTIFF'S BURDEN TO ESTABLISH JURISDICTION AND IT MUST PRESENT EVIDENCE TO IMPLICATE THE ALTER EGO DOCTRINE. (SEE SONORA DIAMOND CORP. V. SUPERIOR COURT (2000) 83 CAL.APP.4TH 523, 539-540.) HERE, THE POSCHL DECLARATION DOES NOT SHOW THAT MR. HYDE HAS SUFFICIENT MINIMUM CONTACTS WITH CALIFORNIA OR IS THE ALTER EGO OF BUDGET PREPAY, INC. SUCH THAT THE COURT MAY EXERCISE JURISDICTION OVER HIM. THIS ACTION IS SOLELY FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT AND PLAINTIFF'S CASES THAT PROVIDE THAT A CORPORATE OFFICER CAN BE LIABLE FOR HIS/HER OWN TORTS AND IT IS PROPER TO EXERCISE JURISDICTION OVER THAT OFFICER ARE INAPPOSITE. (SEE WOLF DESIGNS, INC. V. DHR CO. (C.D. CAL. 2004) 322 F.SUPP.2D 1065, 1072 [" 'UNDER THE FIDUCIARY SHIELD DOCTRINE A PERSON'S MERE ASSOCIATION WITH A CORPORATION THAT CAUSES INJURY IN THE FORUM STATE IS NOT SUFFICIENT IN ITSELF TO PERMIT THAT FORUM TO ASSERT JURISDICTION OVER THE PERSON.'"].) PLAINTIFF CAN SEEK TO DEPOSE MR. HYDE AND SERVE 20 SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND 10 REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION ON HIM, WITH SUCH DISCOVERY LIMITED TO THE ISSUE OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION AND PLAINTIFF'S ALTER EGO CLAIM. (SEE BURDICK V. SUPERIOR COURT (2015) 233 CAL.APP.4TH 8, 30.) THE SUPPLEMENTAL OPPOSITION IS DUE ON JULY 29, 2019 AND THE SUPPLEMENTAL REPLY IS DUE ON AUGUST 9, 2019. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Daniel R. Hyde, Iii To Quash Service Of Summons Based On Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Jul-25-2019 Continued To Nov-12-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-21-2019 Continued To Oct-09-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Daniel R. Hyde, Iii To Quash Service Of Summons Based On Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
Off Calendar May-01-2019 Continued To Aug-19-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Christofer Nolan, Esq., California Public Utilities Commission, State of California, Telephone 415-696-7303, appearing for Plaintiff;
No other appearance.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DANIEL HYDE's Motion Of Daniel R. Hyde, Iii To Quash Service Of Summons Based On Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction.

Defendant Daniel R. Hyde, III's motion to quash is continued to August 19, 2019 to allow for jurisdictional discovery. It is Plaintiff's burden to establish jurisdiction and it must present evidence to implicate the alter ego doctrine. (See Sonora Diamond Corp. v. Superior Court (2000) 83 Cal.App.4th 523, 539-540.) Here, the Poschl declaration does not show that Mr. Hyde has sufficient minimum contacts with California or is the alter ego of Budget Prepay, Inc. such that the Court may exercise jurisdiction over him. This action is solely for breach of contract and Plaintiff's cases that provide that a corporate officer can be liable for his/her own torts and it is proper to exercise jurisdiction over that officer are inapposite. (See Wolf Designs, Inc. v. DHR Co. (C.D. Cal. 2004) 322 F.Supp.2d 1065, 1072 [" 'Under the fiduciary shield doctrine a person's mere association with a corporation that causes injury in the forum state is not sufficient in itself to permit that forum to assert jurisdiction over the person.'"].) Plaintiff can seek to depose Mr. Hyde and serve 20 special interrogatories and 10 requests for production on him, with such discovery limited to the issue of personal jurisdiction and Plaintiff's alter ego claim. (See Burdick v. Superior Court (2015) 233 Cal.App.4th 8, 30.) The supplemental opposition is due on July 29, 2019 and the supplemental reply is due on August 9, 2019.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)
